scope: ISO and shutter custom exposure write safety clamp

crash.observed_problem: An ISO value outside activeFormat.minISO ... activeFormat.maxISO was passed to AVCaptureDevice.
crash.root_cause: Custom exposure write paths did not all sanitize the final ISO and duration immediately before calling AVCaptureDevice; quantization or current device ISO could exceed the current activeFormat after lens or format changes.

runtime_safety.iso_clamp: Final write ISO is clamped with current device.activeFormat.minISO and maxISO.
runtime_safety.invalid_iso: NaN, infinite, or non-positive raw ISO falls back to current device.iso, then minISO if needed.
runtime_safety.duration_clamp: Final write duration is clamped with current activeFormat minExposureDuration and maxExposureDuration.
runtime_safety.invalid_duration: Invalid raw duration falls back to current device.exposureDuration, then minExposureDuration if needed.
runtime_safety.invalid_range: If activeFormat ISO or duration ranges are invalid, the custom exposure write is skipped with a Debug log.

lens_switch_protection.active_format_source: Current AVCaptureDevice.activeFormat is read inside the sessionQueue immediately before each custom exposure write.
lens_switch_protection.old_state_protection: Old UI ticks, pending values, current device ISO, or published ranges cannot bypass the final runtime clamp.

contracts.iso: UI and contract unchanged; runtime write safety added
contracts.shutter: UI and contract unchanged; runtime write safety added

risks: Real-device stress testing is still needed for ISO/Shutter after lens switches.
If a device reports invalid activeFormat ranges during a transition, the current behavior skips the unsafe custom exposure write and logs the reason.
